info.name: Pocket Inspector API
info.description:
# Pocket Inspector API

Backend API for managing door inspections, buildings, sites, and inspector assignments.

## Quick Start
1. Run **Login as Admin** first — tokens are saved automatically
2. Work through folders top to bottom — IDs chain automatically (siteId → buildingId → floorId → doorId)
3. Switch roles using **Login as Inspector** when needed

## Roles
- **ADMIN** — manages everything (sites, buildings, users, certificates, exports)
- **INSPECTOR** — uploads door images and submits inspections

## Response Format
All responses: `{ "data": { ... } }`
All errors: `{ "statusCode", "message", "error", "path", "timestamp" }`

exec: // Save deviceId returned by server (auto-detected from User-Agent)

request.description: Login with admin credentials. Only email and password are needed — device info (deviceId, deviceName, deviceType) is auto-detected from the User-Agent header. The server returns deviceId in the response and it is saved automatically.

exec: // Save deviceId returned by server (auto-detected from User-Agent)

request.description: Login with inspector credentials. Switches accessToken to inspector's token. Saves inspectorToken and inspectorId.
Exchange a refresh token for a new access + refresh token pair. Token is rotated on each call — old refresh token is invalidated immediately.
Returns the profile of whoever is currently logged in (based on the Bearer token).
Shows all devices currently logged in as the current user — deviceName, deviceType, IP address, last used time.
Kicks a specific device. That device gets 401 on its next API request and must log in again.
Logs out from all devices. Use ?keepCurrent=true to stay logged in on the current device.
Revoke the current session. You must log in again after this.

exec: // Re-login as Admin after logout so subsequent requests still work
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ email: adminEmail, password: adminPassword }) }

request.description: Returns the organisation the current user belongs to.
Update the organisation name. Admin only.

exec: // Automatically use admin token for this folder

request.description: Returns all users in the org. Admin only.

exec: // Generate unique email to avoid 409 conflicts

request.description: Create a new user. Set role to ADMIN or INSPECTOR. A random email is generated to avoid conflicts on repeated test runs.
Get a specific user by their ID.
Update a user's name or role.

exec: // Automatically use admin token for this folder

request.description: List all clients in the org. Admin only. Returns clients sorted by name.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Acme Fire Safety Ltd",
  "contactName": "John Smith",
  "contactEmail": "john@acme.com",
  "contactPhone": "+44 20 7946 0958",
  "address": "123 King Street, London EC2V 8AA",
  "notes": "Key account — monthly billing"
}

request.description: Create a new client. Only `name` is required — all other fields are optional. Name must be unique within the org. Saves clientId.
Get a specific client with its linked sites and standalone buildings.
Update client details. Name must remain unique if changed.
Delete a client. This is permanent. Linked sites and buildings will have their clientId set to null.

exec: // Automatically use admin token for this folder

request.description: List all sites. ADMIN sees all sites in the org. INSPECTOR sees only sites they created or are assigned to (via inspection assignment).
Create a new site. Both ADMIN and INSPECTOR can create sites. The site is automatically linked to the creator (createdById). Optional: pass clientId to associate with a client. Saves siteId.
Verifies that an INSPECTOR can create a site. Saves inspectorSiteId. The inspector will only see this site in their own list — admin sees it in the full list.
Get a specific site.
Update site name or notes.
Delete a site. This is permanent.

exec: // Automatically use admin token for this folder

request.description: List all buildings. ADMIN sees all buildings in the org. INSPECTOR sees only buildings they created or are assigned to via an inspection assignment. Filter by site using ?siteId=

Create a standalone building. Both ADMIN and INSPECTOR can create buildings. Optional: pass clientId to associate with a client (only allowed for standalone buildings not linked to a site). Saves buildingId.
Verifies that an INSPECTOR can create a building. Saves inspectorBuildingId. The inspector sees it in their filtered list; admin sees it in the full org list.
Get a specific building.
Update building details.
List all floors for a building.
Inspector approves a building inspection. Sets status: DRAFT → APPROVED. Admin must see APPROVED before uploading the building certificate.
Get a pre-signed GCS URL to upload a PDF certificate. URL expires in 15 minutes — upload immediately.
After uploading to GCS, call this to register the certificate path in the database.
Get a pre-signed URL to download the building certificate PDF.
Delete the building certificate. Removes the PDF from GCS and deletes the certificate record so a new one can be uploaded. Returns 204 No Content on success. Only callable by ADMIN.

exec: // Automatically use admin token for this folder

request.description: Create a floor in a building. Both ADMIN and INSPECTOR can create floors. Saves floorId.
Verifies that an INSPECTOR can create a floor in their own building. Uses inspectorBuildingId. Saves inspectorFloorId.
Get a specific floor.
Update floor label or notes.
Delete a floor.
List all doors on a floor.

exec: // Automatically use admin token for this folder
// Auto-login if no valid admin token

request.description: Create a door on a floor. Both ADMIN and INSPECTOR can create doors. Saves doorId. Status starts as DRAFT.
Verifies that an INSPECTOR can create a door in their own floor. Uses inspectorFloorId. Saves inspectorDoorId.
Get a door with its current status (DRAFT / SUBMITTED / CERTIFIED) and all images.
Update door code or location notes.

STEP 1 of 3 — Request a signed GCS upload URL for a single door image.

Why is 'role' required here?
The role is embedded into the GCS storage path (e.g. orgs/.../images/FRONT_FACE/uuid.jpg). Without knowing the role at this point, the server cannot build the path and therefore cannot generate the signed URL.

Available roles: FRONT_FACE | REAR_FACE | FRAME_GAP | INTUMESCENT_STRIP | SELF_CLOSER | HINGES | SIGNAGE | OTHER

Response returns: { signedUrl, objectPath, imageId, role }

➜ NEXT: Use the returned signedUrl in 'Upload Image to GCS (PUT signed URL)' — PUT the image binary directly to that URL. No Authorization header needed.

➜ THEN: Call 'Register Door Image (after GCS upload)' with the imageId and objectPath to save the record.

STEP 3 of 3 — Register the image in the database after it has been uploaded to GCS.

Use the imageId and objectPath returned from Step 1 (Request Image Upload URL).
'label' is optional — use it to add a human-readable description, especially for OTHER images.

Available roles: FRONT_FACE | REAR_FACE | FRAME_GAP | INTUMESCENT_STRIP | SELF_CLOSER | HINGES | SIGNAGE | OTHER

exec: // If GCS signed-upload failed (no imageId set), use placeholder values
// so Register Image can still run and allow door submission

item.name: Upload Image to GCS (PUT signed URL)

exec: // This request must be run manually — paste the signedUrl from Step 1 into the {{signedUploadUrl}} env variable.
// Skipped automatically during Newman runs when no real URL is set.
// Remove Authorization header — GCS signed URLs must NOT receive a Bearer token.

header.description: Must exactly match the contentType you passed to signed-upload (e.g. image/jpeg, image/png, image/heic)

request.description:
STEP 2 of 3 — Upload the image file directly to Google Cloud Storage using the signed URL.

How to use this request:
1. Copy the 'signedUrl' value from the 'Request Image Upload URL' response
2. Paste it into the request URL above (replace {{signedUploadUrl}} or set it as an environment variable)
3. Set Content-Type header to match what you requested (e.g. image/jpeg)
4. In Body tab → select 'binary' → click 'Select File' → pick your image file
5. Send — GCS returns 200 OK with an empty body if successful

⚠️  IMPORTANT: Do NOT add an Authorization header here. The signed URL itself is the authentication. Adding a Bearer token will cause a 403 error.

⚠️  The signed URL expires in 15 minutes. If you get a 403, go back to Step 1 and get a new URL.

➜ NEXT: Call 'Register Door Image (after GCS upload)' with the imageId and objectPath from Step 1 to save the record in the database.

STEP 1 of 3 (Batch) — Request signed GCS upload URLs for up to 10 door images in a single call.

Why is 'role' required per image?
The role is embedded into each image's GCS storage path (e.g. orgs/.../images/FRONT_FACE/uuid.jpg). Without it the server cannot build the path or generate the signed URL.

Available roles: FRONT_FACE | REAR_FACE | FRAME_GAP | INTUMESCENT_STRIP | SELF_CLOSER | HINGES | SIGNAGE | OTHER

Response returns an array of: { signedUrl, objectPath, imageId, role }

➜ NEXT: For each item in the response, PUT the image binary to its signedUrl. See 'Upload Image to GCS (PUT signed URL)'. Run all PUTs in parallel for best performance — no Authorization header on any of them.

➜ THEN: Call 'Batch Register Image URLs' with all imageId + objectPath pairs to save all records in one request.

exec: pm.test('Batch signed URLs returned — ' + json.data.length + ' items', () => {

item.name: Batch Register Image URLs (after GCS uploads)

request.description:
STEP 3 of 3 (Batch) — Register all images in the database after uploading them to GCS.

Replace each <imageId-N> and <objectPath-N> with the values returned from 'Batch Request Image Upload URLs' (Step 1). All records are saved in a single database transaction.

'label' is optional on each image — use it to add a human-readable description, especially useful for OTHER images.

Available roles: FRONT_FACE | REAR_FACE | FRAME_GAP | INTUMESCENT_STRIP | SELF_CLOSER | HINGES | SIGNAGE | OTHER

Returns all images for the door, each with:
- `downloadUrl` + `downloadUrlExpiresAt` — signed URL for the original (15 min)
- `downloadUrlThumb` + `downloadUrlThumbExpiresAt` — signed URL for the 400×400 JPEG thumbnail (7 days)

**Client caching strategy:**
Cache URLs keyed by `imageId`. Check `downloadUrlExpiresAt` before use — re-fetch this list (or call the single-image signed-download endpoint) if expiring within 60 seconds.

exec: // Use last image for delete so imageId stays valid for signed-download

request.description:
Fetches fresh signed download URLs for a single image.

Use this when the cached `downloadUrl` from `List Door Images` is about to expire (check `downloadUrlExpiresAt` — refresh 60 s before expiry).

Returns:
- `downloadUrl` + `downloadUrlExpiresAt` (15 min expiry)
- `downloadUrlThumb` + `downloadUrlThumbExpiresAt` (7 day expiry)
- `role` and `label`

Permanently deletes 1–20 door images in a single call.

**What happens:**
1. Validates all imageIds belong to this door and org
2. Deletes original + thumbnail files from GCS (best-effort)
3. Removes DB records in a single transaction
4. Writes an `DOOR_IMAGE_DELETED` audit log entry for each image (with full metadata snapshot)

**Access:** Admin can delete any image. Inspector can only delete images they uploaded.

**To get imageIds:** call `List Door Images` first and copy the `id` fields.
Inspector marks door as inspected. Requires at least 1 registered image. Changes status: DRAFT → SUBMITTED.
Get a pre-signed GCS URL to upload a door certificate PDF.
Register the uploaded door certificate.
Get a signed URL to download the door certificate PDF.

Delete the door certificate. Removes the PDF from GCS, deletes the certificate record, and resets the door status from CERTIFIED back to SUBMITTED so a new certificate can be uploaded. Returns 204 No Content on success. Only callable by ADMIN.

exec: // Ensure admin token is used for door folder
// Create a fresh floor for door tests (since the Floors folder deleted floorId)
if (!buildingId) return; // Skip if no building created
// Only create if first request in this folder (avoid re-creating per request)
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ buildingId, label: 'D', notes: 'Floor for door tests' }) }

request.description: List all inspection jobs in the org.
Create a new inspection job for a site or building. Saves inspectionId.
Get full inspection details including assignments.
Assign an inspector to the inspection job. Inspector receives a notification.
Inspector accepts or declines the assignment. Send status: ACCEPTED or DECLINED.

exec: // Switch to inspector token for this request

request.description: Close and archive a completed inspection.

exec: // Use admin token for inspection folder by default
pm.test('Export job created — exportJobId saved', () => pm.response.to.have.status(201));

request.description: Start a background job to build a ZIP of all inspection data. Saves exportJobId.
Check status: QUEUED → RUNNING → DONE (or FAILED). Poll every few seconds.
Get a signed URL to download the ZIP. Only works when status is DONE.

exec: // Automatically use admin token for this folder

request.description: Register a Firebase push notification token for this device. Call after login on mobile apps.

exec: // Automatically use inspector token for this folder

item.name: 📋 Surveys
item.description: Survey versioning — each building has numbered survey cycles (v1, v2, v3…). When confirmed complete, a survey is frozen and read-only. Start a new survey to clone the floor/door structure for the next cycle.

request.description: List all survey versions for a building in version order (oldest first). Use this for the Survey History tab. Saves surveyId from the first result.

exec: pm.test('No active survey (404 expected on completed buildings)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(404));

request.description: Get the current ACTIVE survey for a building. Returns 404 if no active survey exists (all surveys are completed and start-next hasn't been called yet). Saves currentSurveyId.
Get full read-only detail of any survey (active or completed). Includes all floors and doors with statuses and image/cert counts. Use surveyId saved from List Survey History.

item.name: Get Historical Building Cert Download URL

request.description: Get a signed download URL for the building certificate of a specific (historical) survey. Use in the history detail view.

exec: console.log('Survey v' + json.data.version + ' is now COMPLETED');
console.log('Error (expected if pre-conditions not met):', json.message);

request.description: Mark the current ACTIVE survey as COMPLETED (admin only). Pre-conditions: Building.status must be CERTIFIED (building cert uploaded) AND all doors must be CERTIFIED. Optional: schedule the next survey in the same call. All scheduling fields are optional — send empty body {} to confirm without scheduling.

exec: console.log('Survey v' + json.data.version + ' started — cloned ' + json.data.floorsCloned + ' floors, ' + json.data.doorsCloned + ' doors');

request.description: Start a new survey cycle (admin only). Clones all floors and doors from the last completed survey — images and certificates are NOT copied. Resets Building.status to DRAFT. Requires no ACTIVE survey to currently exist (confirm-complete first). Saves new currentSurveyId.

Update scheduling metadata on the current active survey (admin only). All fields are optional. Pass null to clear a field. If nextAssignedInspectorId is provided, a push notification is sent to that inspector.
Check server and database health. Returns { status: 'ok' } when everything is running.

exec: // deviceId is auto-assigned by the server on login and saved from the response.
// This fallback is only used if login hasn't run yet (e.g. mid-collection run).
